name: Circle CCTP TokenMessenger
notes: CCTP v1 burn entrypoint. depositForBurn(amount, destinationDomain, mintRecipient, burnToken) — destinationDomain is Circle's domain id; mintRecipient is the dest recipient. Verified on-chain (recent txs all depositForBurn).

notes: CCTP v1 burn entrypoint (Avalanche). Verified on-chain.

name: deBridgeGate (DLN)
notes: Main entry point for deBridge cross-chain transfers. Order hash extractable from the createOrder event.

name: Hop Protocol: L1 Bridge
notes: Hop L1 bridge entrypoint — locks tokens for L2 mint

name: Socket / Bungee Gateway
notes: Aggregator — routes through underlying bridges. Check the inner transactions for actual bridge used.

name: Polygon: RootChainManager
notes: Canonical Polygon PoS bridge

name: Polygon: ERC20Predicate
notes: Polygon canonical ERC-20 deposit gate (lockTokens)

name: Arbitrum: Inbox
notes: Arbitrum One canonical Inbox — ETH deposits + L1→L2 messages

name: Arbitrum: DelayedInbox
notes: Arbitrum One DelayedInbox — canonical L1 entry point for forced inclusion txs (v0.32.1 audit).

name: zkSync Era: L1ERC20Bridge
notes: zkSync Era L1ERC20Bridge — canonical L1 entry point for ERC-20 deposits to zkSync Era (v0.32.1 audit).

name: Multichain / Anyswap (defunct)
notes: Multichain ceased operations July 2023 after the founder's arrest in China. Historical contracts retained for legacy investigations — funds bridged via Multichain after the shutdown are likely unrecoverable.

name: LayerZero: Endpoint v1
notes: Generic LayerZero messaging endpoint. Many OFTs / aggregators route through this; check inner OApp for the actual destination.

name: Chainlink CCIP: Router (Ethereum)
notes: Chainlink Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol router. ccipSend() encodes destChainSelector + receiver in calldata.

name: Squid Router
notes: Squid aggregator on top of Axelar GMP. Check Axelarscan for the underlying GMP message.

name: Hyperliquid: Bridge2 (Arbitrum)
notes: Canonical USDC deposit gateway on Arbitrum for Hyperliquid L1. Withdrawals signed by Hyperliquid validator set.

_section: Arbitrum-side bridges (added v0.28.0 — Zigha trace coverage fix). Pre-v0.28 bridges.json had ONE Arbitrum entry (Hyperliquid). identify_cross_chain_handoffs keys on (chain, address), so any transfer on Arbitrum to a bridge contract missed detection. The Zigha case (Arbitrum→Ethereum DeBridge / 1inch consolidation) was invisible. The entries below add Arbitrum-side coverage. See docs/TRACE_COVERAGE_DIAGNOSIS_ZIGHA.md.

name: deBridgeGate (DLN) on Arbitrum
notes: DeBridge Gate deploys deterministically at the same address on all supported chains. The order hash is extractable from the createOrder event. Zigha unblocker — the v0.27.x worker missed DeBridge handoffs entirely on the Arbitrum side.
_audit_status: internally_corroborated: same address as pre-v0.28 Ethereum entry (deterministic deploy across EVM chains; cross-referenced in v0.28.4 audit)

name: deBridge DLN Source on Arbitrum
notes: DLN Source deploys at the same address on multiple chains (deterministic deploy). The Zigha consolidation funnel went through this on Arbitrum.
_audit_status: internally_corroborated: same address as pre-v0.28 Ethereum entry (deterministic deploy across EVM chains; cross-referenced in v0.28.4 audit)

_section: 1inch Aggregation Router v5/v6 (0x1111111254eeb25477b68fb85ed929f73a960582, 0x111111125421ca6dc452d289314280a0f8842a65) is correctly cataloged in defi_protocols.json as a DEX/swap router, not a bridge. The bridge_calldata.py _decode_1inch stub recognizes 1inch method IDs for surfacing in trace reports, but the address itself is NOT in bridges.json — 1inch's primary function is same-chain DEX aggregation. Fusion+ cross-chain is a layered protocol; over-classifying the base router would tag the vast majority of same-chain 1inch txs as bridges.

name: Stargate: Router on Arbitrum
notes: Stargate Router on Arbitrum One — Stargate composer for cross-chain stablecoin swaps via LayerZero.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v0284: Stargate Router Arbitrum — confirmed via WebFetch from stargateprotocol.gitbook.io (v0.28.4 audit)

name: Stargate: Router ETH on Arbitrum
notes: Stargate native-ETH router on Arbitrum One.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v0284: Stargate Router ETH Arbitrum — confirmed via WebFetch from stargateprotocol.gitbook.io (v0.28.4 audit)

name: Across V3: SpokePool on Arbitrum
notes: Across V3 SpokePool on Arbitrum One. Optimistic relayer model — funds bridged in minutes, settled in ~2h.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v0284: Across V3 SpokePool Arbitrum — confirmed via WebFetch from docs.across.to (v0.28.4 audit)

name: Wormhole: Token Bridge on Arbitrum

name: LayerZero: Endpoint v1 on Arbitrum

_audit_status: externally_verified_v0284: LayerZero V1 Endpoint Arbitrum — confirmed via WebSearch + arbiscan.io (v0.28.4 audit). Same address deploys on Arb/Op/BSC/AVAX.

name: Hop Protocol: L2 AMM Wrapper on Arbitrum
notes: Hop L2 AMM wrapper deploys at the same address across L2s — note this matches the Ethereum-side bridge entry. Confidence: medium because the L2 deployment pattern shifted between Hop v1 / v2.
_audit_status: internally_corroborated: same address as pre-v0.28 Ethereum entry (deterministic deploy across EVM chains; cross-referenced in v0.28.4 audit)

name: SunSwap: Smart Router (Tron)
notes: SunSwap Smart Router on Tron mainnet — routes USDT-TRC20 and USDD swaps through V1/V2/V3 pools + PSM + SunCurve. Pre-v0.31.2 bridges.json had zero Tron-keyed entries, so TRC-20 stablecoin laundering flows that touched a SunSwap router surfaced as unlabeled EOAs. Tron's bridge ecosystem is structurally thin (Wormhole, Stargate, PolyNetwork are NOT deployed on Tron — externally confirmed v0.31.2), so the DEX-aggregator layer functionally serves as Tron's primary cross-asset routing hop.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031_2: WebFetch from https://github.com/sun-protocol/smart-exchange-router (deployment example mainnet address); cross-referenced WebSearch SUN.io zendesk announcement on Smart Router contract

name: JustLend: jTRX Market (Tron)
notes: JustLend jTRX market on Tron mainnet — primary TRX lending pool in Tron's JUST DeFi stack. Stablecoin laundering on Tron routinely touches JustLend (deposit USDT → borrow TRX → swap → re-deposit) so flagging it as bridge-adjacent surfaces the otherwise-invisible TRC-20 → JustLend → TRX hop. Labeled `category: bridge` (not `defi_protocol`) because v0.30.x trace bridge-following infrastructure only follows category=bridge edges; promoting jTRX into bridges.json puts it on the BFS path.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031_2: WebSearch returned tronscan.org/#/token20/TE2RzoSV3wFK99w6J9UnnZ4vLfXYoxvRwP as the canonical jTRX page

name: USDD: Peg Stability Module (Tron)
notes: USDD Peg Stability Module on Tron — 1:1 swaps between USDD and USDT/USDC/TUSD/USDJ. Cross-stablecoin laundering hop on Tron: USDT-TRC20 → USDD → USDT-ERC20 (post-bridge) defeats single-asset blacklist enforcement. Confidence medium because USDD 2.0 redeployed in 2024 with new contract addresses — this is the post-2.0 PSM address per USDD docs but a second-level on-chain audit is recommended before relying on it for high-stakes freezing decisions.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031_2: WebSearch USDD docs / Eco support referenced TXDk8mbtRbXeYuMNS83CfKPaYYT8XWv9Hz as the new USDD contract address post-2.0; need direct Tronscan verification once API access is restored

name: Wormhole: Token Bridge (Solana)
notes: Wormhole Token Bridge program on Solana mainnet — also the canonical Portal Bridge entry point (same program ID). Pre-v0.31.2 bridges.json had zero Solana-keyed entries, so Solana-originated bridge handoffs (the high-value SOL/USDC outflow path for stablecoin theft post-2023) surfaced as unlabeled SPL program calls. Address verified against Wormhole's own SDK constants in sdk/js/src/utils/consts.ts on the main branch.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031_2: WebFetch from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wormhole-foundation/wormhole/main/sdk/js/src/utils/consts.ts confirmed token bridge address wormDTUJ6AWPNvk59vGQbDvGJmqbDTdgWgAqcLBCgUb on Solana mainnet

name: Wormhole: Core Bridge (Solana)
notes: Wormhole Core Bridge (guardian-signed message layer) on Solana mainnet. Every Wormhole-mediated SPL transfer crosses this program before / after the Token Bridge — flagging both gives the trace both endpoints of the cross-chain hop. Address verified against Wormhole's own SDK constants.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031_2: WebFetch from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wormhole-foundation/wormhole/main/sdk/js/src/utils/consts.ts confirmed core bridge address worm2ZoG2kUd4vFXhvjh93UUH596ayRfgQ2MgjNMTth on Solana mainnet

name: deBridge: DLN Program (Solana)
notes: deBridge main program on Solana mainnet — paired with the existing 0x43dE2d77BF8027e25dBD179B491e8d64f38398aA (deBridgeGate DLN on Ethereum) entry. Pre-v0.31.2, a Solana-origin deBridge handoff resolved only on the EVM side, so the trace lost the SPL → EVM transition.
_audit_status: externally_verified_v031_2: WebSearch returned debridge-finance/debridge-solana-sdk and docs.debridge.com pages referencing DEbrdGj3HsRsAzx6uH4MKyREKxVAfBydijLUF3ygsFfh as the main Solana program ID

name: Polygon zkEVM: L1 Bridge
notes: Polygon zkEVM PolygonZkEVMBridge — Ethereum-L1 entry point for the zkEVM rollup (bridgeAsset / bridgeMessage). Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' Hop-1 (Polygon zkEVM canonical bridge escape).
_audit_status: v0_32_1_corrected: the prior round-2 wire-up pasted the Polygon PoS ERC20Predicate address (0x40ec5b33…) by mistake, colliding with the existing 'Polygon: ERC20Predicate' entry. Replaced with the canonical PolygonZkEVMBridge L1 address. Operator should still confirm against polygonscan.com before freeze decisions (verified=false).

name: Linea: L1 Bridge
notes: Linea Consensys L1 message service / token bridge entry point on Ethereum. Decodes via the OP-Stack ABI family (depositERC20{To}, depositETH{To}) as Linea reuses the standard-bridge calldata shape. Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' (Linea variant).
_audit_status: operator_unverified_v0_32_1_round2: address as provided by round-2 wire-up prompt; operator should re-confirm against Consensys / linea.build docs.

name: Scroll: L1 Gateway Router
notes: Scroll L1 Gateway Router — Ethereum-side entry point for the Scroll zkEVM rollup. Decodes via the OP-Stack ABI family (Scroll exposes a depositERC20-shaped calldata). Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' (Scroll variant).
_audit_status: operator_unverified_v0_32_1_round2: address as provided by round-2 wire-up prompt; operator should re-confirm against scroll.io docs.

name: Blast: L1 Bridge
notes: Blast L1 Bridge — Ethereum-side entry point. Decodes via the OP-Stack ABI family (Blast is OP-Stack derivative). Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' (Blast variant).
_audit_status: operator_unverified_v0_32_1_round2: address as provided by round-2 wire-up prompt; operator should re-confirm against blast.io docs.

name: opBNB: L1 Bridge
notes: opBNB L1 Bridge — BSC-side entry point for the opBNB OP-Stack L2. Decodes via OP-Stack ABI family. NB: chain is bsc, not ethereum — opBNB anchors to BSC rather than Ethereum. Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' (opBNB variant).
_audit_status: operator_unverified_v0_32_1_round2: address as provided by round-2 wire-up prompt; operator should re-confirm against opbnb.bnbchain.org docs.

name: Mantle: L1 Bridge
notes: Mantle L1 Bridge — Ethereum-side native bridge for the Mantle Network L2. Decodes via the OP-Stack ABI family (Mantle is BitDAO/Mantle OP-Stack derivative). Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' (Mantle variant).
_audit_status: operator_unverified_v0_32_1_round2: address as provided by round-2 wire-up prompt; operator should re-confirm against mantle.xyz docs.

name: Manta Pacific: L1 Bridge
notes: Manta Pacific L1 Bridge — Ethereum-side native bridge for the Manta Pacific OP-Stack L2. Decodes via the OP-Stack ABI family. Closes round-2 adversary audit Route 1' (Manta variant).
_audit_status: operator_unverified_v0_32_1_round2: address as provided by round-2 wire-up prompt; operator should re-confirm against pacific.manta.network docs.

name: Orbiter Finance Maker (0x095D...26c9)
notes: Orbiter Finance cross-rollup bridge Maker (EOA model): senders transfer directly to this externally-owned address (no contract call); the destination network is encoded in the final digits of the transfer amount (Orbiter internalId) and the Maker repays the SAME sender address on the destination chain, so continuation is recoverable via same-address lock-and-mint matching. Address from Orbiter-Finance/orbiter-sdk maker_list.mainnet.ts (makerAddress field), confirmed on-chain as an EOA with a bridge-scale outbound nonce. Orbiter routes: arbitrum, mainnet, optimism, polygon, starknet, zksync.

notes: Orbiter Finance cross-rollup bridge Maker (EOA model): senders transfer directly to this externally-owned address (no contract call); the destination network is encoded in the final digits of the transfer amount (Orbiter internalId) and the Maker repays the SAME sender address on the destination chain, so continuation is recoverable via same-address lock-and-mint matching. Address from Orbiter-Finance/orbiter-sdk maker_list.mainnet.ts (makerAddress field), confirmed on-chain as an EOA with a bridge-scale outbound nonce. Orbiter routes: arbitrum, bnbchain, boba, immutableX, loopring, mainnet, metis, nova, optimism, polygon, starknet, zkspace, zksync.

name: Allbridge Core Bridge

name: Mayan Forwarder
notes: Mayan Forwarder — the unified entry point for Mayan cross-chain swaps (Swift / MCTP / Wormhole-Swap), deployed at the same address on every supported EVM chain. On-chain confirmed as a deployed contract. Mayan settles via Wormhole; follow up on the Mayan explorer.

name: THORChain Router
notes: THORChain native cross-chain SWAP router (Ethereum). Funds sent here are swapped to a DIFFERENT asset on a DIFFERENT chain and delivered to a DIFFERENT recipient specified in the swap memo (calldata) — NOT a same-address lock-and-mint, so the destination asset/chain/recipient must be read from the deposit/transferOut memo. Address from the THORNode inbound_addresses endpoint (the router is stable; asgard vaults rotate and are intentionally NOT labeled). Confirmed on-chain as a deployed contract (8558 bytes). Routers on BSC/AVAX/Base require the inbound_addresses API to enumerate (not reachable from this build env).

name: THORChain Router v4.1.1
notes: CURRENT THORChain Router (v4.1.1, Etherscan-labeled). The v0.34 seed only had the legacy v2 router (0xc145…c2ce); live swaps route through this v4 router, so detection of present-day THORChain laundering requires this entry. Cross-chain destination (incl. native BTC) is in the depositWithExpiry/deposit MEMO — decoded by trace/bridge_calldata._decode_thorchain. VERIFIED on-chain: tx 0x0e9a9c2e6d172724bfcee3e81f973b62bb396df82ccb3932db3f5caae733a721 is a depositWithExpiry with memo '=:BTC.BTC:bc1q8w2ypqgx39gucxcypqv2m90wz9rvhmmrcnpdjs:117760'; the decoder extracts destination_chain=bitcoin + that address at high confidence (see tests/fixtures/thorchain_btc_swap.json).

name: THORChain Router v3.0.1
notes: THORChain Router v3.0.1 (Etherscan-labeled). Intermediate router version between the legacy v2 (0xc145…c2ce) and current v4.1.1 (0xd37b…7146); historical cases may have routed through it. Same depositWithExpiry/deposit MEMO destination encoding decoded by _decode_thorchain.
